brute_force.label: Brute Force
brute_force.log_source: Authentication logs (Windows Security 4625, Linux auth.log, Okta, Azure AD SigninLogs)
false_positives: Shared service accounts with expired passwords
coverage_gaps: Slow, low-and-slow brute force spread over days
Distributed brute force from many source IPs
Offline password attacks against exfiltrated hashes
improvements: Add geo-location and impossible travel enrichment
Correlate with threat intelligence IP reputation
Weight by asset criticality
Incorporate a user risk score baseline

credential_access.label: Credential Access
credential_access.log_source: Endpoint (Sysmon 10, EDR process/memory access), Windows Security logs
false_positives: Legitimate backup or AV software accessing LSASS memory
coverage_gaps: In-memory-only dumping tools with custom binaries
Living-off-the-land credential access techniques
Encrypted or fileless dump exfiltration
improvements: Add process ancestry / parent-child correlation
Enrich with EDR memory access alerts
Track access to LSASS handle requests with specific access masks

malware.label: Malware
malware.log_source: EDR/AV alerts, Sysmon (Event ID 1, 7, 11), process creation logs
false_positives: Security testing tools (EICAR, custom pentest payloads)
coverage_gaps: Zero-day or previously unseen malware without known hashes
Fileless malware executing solely in memory
Polymorphic malware that changes signature per infection
improvements: Add YARA rule based content inspection
Enrich hashes with threat intel reputation feeds
Add behavioral sandboxing detonation results

powershell.label: PowerShell
powershell.log_source: PowerShell Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104), Sysmon Event ID 1
false_positives: Legitimate IT automation and configuration management scripts
coverage_gaps: Heavily obfuscated scripts using custom encoding beyond base64
PowerShell run via alternate hosts (e.g. powershell.exe renamed)
Downgrade attacks that bypass script block logging entirely
improvements: Decode base64 command blocks automatically for inspection
Correlate with network connections initiated post-execution
Baseline normal admin PowerShell usage per host

lateral_movement.label: Lateral Movement
lateral_movement.log_source: Windows Security (4624 Type 3/10), Sysmon Network (3), firewall/NetFlow logs
false_positives: IT administrators performing routine remote maintenance
coverage_gaps: Movement over uncommon or encrypted protocols
Living-off-the-land lateral movement using native tools
Movement within a single broadcast domain not crossing monitored chokepoints
improvements: Build a baseline of normal host-to-host communication graphs
Correlate with prior reconnaissance or credential access alerts
Add asset criticality weighting for crown-jewel systems

persistence.label: Persistence
persistence.log_source: Windows Security (4698, 4657), Sysmon (Event ID 13), Registry/Task Scheduler logs
false_positives: Legitimate software installers creating scheduled tasks
coverage_gaps: Persistence via less common autostart extensibility points (ASEPs)
Persistence hidden in WMI repository without file artifacts
Cloud-native persistence (e.g. serverless function triggers)
improvements: Maintain an allowlist of known-good scheduled tasks and services
Correlate creation events with the parent process reputation
Add periodic ASEP baseline diffing

ransomware.label: Ransomware
ransomware.log_source: File integrity monitoring, Sysmon (Event ID 11, 23), EDR file activity telemetry
false_positives: Legitimate bulk file compression or archiving jobs
Backup software rewriting large file sets
coverage_gaps: Slow encryption designed to stay under volumetric thresholds
Ransomware that encrypts only a small set of high-value files
Double-extortion actors who exfiltrate before encrypting, bypassing pure encryption-rate detection
improvements: Add shadow copy and backup deletion correlation
Monitor file entropy changes rather than just rename volume
Add canary/honeyfile triggers across shares

phishing.label: Phishing
phishing.log_source: Email gateway logs, Microsoft 365/Google Workspace audit logs, proxy logs
false_positives: Legitimate marketing emails resembling phishing patterns
Newly registered but legitimate business domains
coverage_gaps: Spear-phishing with no attachment or link (pure social engineering)
Phishing hosted on compromised legitimate domains
Multi-stage phishing where the malicious payload is delivered later
improvements: Integrate with URL sandboxing/detonation services
Add sender domain age and reputation enrichment
Correlate with subsequent user click and authentication events

privilege_escalation.label: Privilege Escalation
privilege_escalation.log_source: Windows Security (4672, 4732), cloud IAM audit logs (AWS CloudTrail, Azure AD Audit)
false_positives: Approved change-management driven group membership changes
coverage_gaps: Privilege escalation via undocumented software vulnerabilities
Escalation using legitimate but overly-permissive existing roles
Escalation chains spanning multiple cloud accounts
improvements: Correlate with just-in-time access approval workflows
Add anomaly detection for rarely-used privileged roles
Flag privilege changes outside change windows

data_exfiltration.label: Data Exfiltration
data_exfiltration.log_source: Proxy/firewall logs, DLP alerts, cloud storage audit logs, NetFlow
false_positives: Scheduled legitimate backups to cloud storage
coverage_gaps: Exfiltration disguised within normal encrypted business traffic
Slow low-volume exfiltration over long periods
Exfiltration via removable media without endpoint DLP coverage
improvements: Add destination reputation and geolocation enrichment
Baseline per-user/per-department normal transfer volume
Correlate with prior staging or compression activity

web_attacks.label: Web Attacks
web_attacks.log_source: Web server/access logs, WAF logs, reverse proxy logs
false_positives: Automated vulnerability scanners run by the security team
coverage_gaps: Zero-day exploits with no known signature
Attacks encoded to evade WAF pattern matching
Slow, single-request exploitation with no volumetric signal
improvements: Add WAF rule correlation and payload decoding
Enrich with source IP threat intelligence
Track sequential attack chains (recon -> exploit -> webshell)

cloud_attacks.label: Cloud Attacks
cloud_attacks.log_source: AWS CloudTrail, Azure AD SigninLogs, GCP Audit Logs, Kubernetes audit logs
false_positives: Legitimate administrative activity from new locations
coverage_gaps: Abuse of legitimate, narrowly-scoped API permissions
Multi-cloud attack chains not correlated across providers
Actions performed via compromised but valid service principal credentials
improvements: Add impossible-travel and device-trust enrichment for cloud logins
Baseline normal API call patterns per identity
Correlate IAM changes with subsequent resource access

living_off_the_land.label: Living Off The Land
false_positives: Legitimate IT administration using native utilities
Software installers invoking regsvr32/rundll32 as designed
Vendor management tools using bitsadmin/certutil for updates
coverage_gaps: Novel or rare LOLBins not yet catalogued
Chained LOLBin execution split across multiple low-signal steps
LOLBin usage on non-Windows platforms (Linux/macOS binaries)
improvements: Correlate LOLBin usage with unusual parent processes
Add command-line argument anomaly scoring
Cross-reference with LOLBAS project indicators

reconnaissance.label: Reconnaissance
reconnaissance.log_source: Firewall/IDS logs, DNS logs, Windows Security (4661, 4662)
false_positives: Authorized vulnerability scanning by the security team
Network monitoring tools performing health checks
coverage_gaps: Slow, distributed scanning below volumetric thresholds
Passive reconnaissance with no active network signal
Reconnaissance performed through legitimate cloud APIs
improvements: Correlate scanning source with subsequent exploitation attempts
Add allowlisting for known scanner IP ranges
Track scan pattern diversity (ports/hosts touched) rather than raw volume
